Logic Analysis: Contains Patient class with __init__ and to_dict method. No dependencies on other project files.
Contains Database class with methods: __init__, create_tables, insert_patient, update_patient, delete_patient, fetch_all, close. Depends on models.py (Patient).
Contains TriageRuleEngine class with __init__, assign_urgency, load_rules. Depends on a JSON rule file (external). No direct dependency on other project files.
Contains QueueManager class with methods: __init__, add_patient, dequeue, re_triage, get_queue, get_patient, _sort_queue, save_state, load_state. Depends on models.py, database.py, triage_rules.py.
Contains DashboardAPI class with __init__, get_queue_data, get_stats. Depends on queue_manager.py.

Contains FastAPIApp class with start, register_routes, on_event handlers, and route functions (post_register, post_dequeue, post_re_triage, get_queue). Depends on triage_rules.py, queue_manager.py, dashboard.py.
Frontend HTML page. No backend dependencies, but communicates via REST API.
Frontend JavaScript for polling and UI updates. Depends on index.html and style.css.
Full API spec:
openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: Hospital Triage Queue System API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /register:
    post:
      summary: Register a new patient
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                name:
                  type: string
                symptoms:
                  type: string
                manual_urgency:
                  type: integer
                  nullable: true
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Patient registered successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Patient'
  /dequeue:
    post:
      summary: Dequeue the highest priority patient
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Patient removed from queue
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Patient'
  /re_triage:
    post:
      summary: Update urgency for a patient
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                patient_id:
                  type: integer
                new_urgency:
                  type: integer
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Patient urgency updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Patient'
  /queue:
    get:
      summary: Get current queue with waiting times
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of patients in queue
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/Patient'
  /stats:
    get:
      summary: Get queue statistics
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Statistics object
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  total_patients:
                    type: integer
                  average_wait_time:
                    type: number
components:
  schemas:
    Patient:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: integer
        name:
          type: string
        symptoms:
          type: string
        urgency:
          type: integer
        triage_time:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        arrival_time:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        waiting_seconds:
          type: integer
Shared Knowledge: The symptom-to-urgency mapping is defined in `triage_rules.py` using a configurable JSON file (default `rules.json`). The mapping follows the ESI (Emergency Severity Index) standard. The `QueueManager` uses an `asyncio.Lock` to protect queue mutations. The database file path is `triage.db` by default, configurable via environment variable. The frontend polls `GET /queue` every 500ms to update the dashboard.
Anything UNCLEAR: The symptom-to-urgency mapping algorithm requires a clinical basis. We will implement a default rule set based on common ESI indicators (e.g., chest pain -> 1, severe abdominal pain -> 2, minor laceration -> 4) and make the rules configurable via a JSON file. Multi-user concurrency is handled via an asyncio lock on the QueueManager, but in a distributed deployment a database-level lock or optimistic concurrency would be needed. For this prototype, single-instance is assumed.
